name: TextField
purpose: Allows users to enter and edit single-line text data.
whenToUse: Collecting short, single-line text (e.g., names, emails, passwords)
whenNotToUse: Collecting multi-line text (use TextField with maxLines > 1)
Selecting from a predefined list (use DropdownButton or similar)

accessibility.semantics: Ensure the TextField is wrapped in appropriate Semantics if custom behavior is added.
accessibility.label: Use decoration labelText to provide context for screen readers. Do not rely solely on hintText as a replacement for a proper label.
accessibility.error: Provide clear errorText in InputDecoration so it is announced by assistive technologies.
accessibility.disabled: Set enabled to false to properly communicate the disabled state to the accessibility tree.

flutterMappingNotes: Map tokenBindings to InputDecoration properties (e.g., fillColor, enabledBorder, focusedBorder, errorBorder, disabledBorder).
Use OutlineInputBorder to apply border radius and color tokens.
Map typography tokens to TextStyle for the 'style' and 'decoration.hintStyle' properties.
Hover states can be managed via MouseRegion or by relying on InputDecoration.hoverColor if using a filled decoration.
Hover state is primarily applicable to Flutter desktop and web platforms, rather than primary mobile touch usage.
